v_pr_title: Pfizer Provides Update on Phase 3 Inclacumab Study for the Treatment of People with Sickle Cell Disease
v_pr_key_info: Pfizer Provides Update on Phase 3 Inclacumab Study for the Treatment of People with Sickle Cell Disease Friday, August 15, 2025 - 06:45am Share NEW YORK, Friday, August 15, 2025 – Pfizer Inc. (NYSE: PFE) today announced results from the Phase 3 THRIVE-131 study evaluating inclacumab, an investigational P-selectin inhibitor, in patients 16 years of age and older with sickle cell disease (SCD). The study did not meet its primary endpoint of significant reduction in the rate of vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs) in participants receiving inclacumab versus placebo every 12 weeks over 48 weeks. Inclacuma
v_summary: Pfizer announced Phase 3 THRIVE-131 results for inclacumab (NCT04935879) on August 15, 2025, not June 6, 2024; the trial failed to meet its primary endpoint of reducing vaso-occlusive crises in sickle cell disease patients.

catalyst_summary: Wave Life Sciences announced interim Phase 1 clinical trial results for WVE-007 in the INLIGHT trial in December 2025, showing a single 240 mg dose achieved fat loss comparable to GLP-1 at three months with muscle preservation.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study of Ascending Doses of WVE-007 to Evaluate Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics in Adults Living With Overweight or Obesity

catalyst_summary: NervGen Pharma announced positive topline data from the chronic cohort of their Phase 1b/2a trial of NVG-291 for spinal cord injury, meeting the primary endpoint of statistical significance on motor evoked potential amplitude, though the exact announcement date is not specified in available sources and predates expectations for early June 2025.[1][2]
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 1b/2a Study of NVG-291 in Spinal Cord Injury Subjects (CONNECT)
ct_conditions: Spinal Cord Injuries; Chronic Spinal Cord Injury; Subacute Spinal Cord Injury

catalyst_summary: C4 Therapeutics (CCCC) shared monotherapy clinical data for cemsidomide in multiple myeloma in December 2023, demonstrating immune cell activation at clinically relevant doses, around the time of their oncology deal announcement on 2023-12-13.[7][8][9]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2 Open-Label Multi-Center Study to Characterize the Safety and Tolerability of CFT7455 in Subjects With Relapsed/Refractory Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma or Multiple Myeloma

catalyst_summary: FDA approved Eli Lilly's Omvoh (mirikizumab) for Crohn's disease on October 14, 2024, based on positive Phase III VIVID-1 trial results showing 53% clinical remission versus 36% on placebo at one year.[3]
ct_official_title: VOICE-Characterization of Early Response to Vedolizumab and IL-23 Antagonists in Participants With Crohn's Disease Using Patient-Reported Outcome Measures: A Prospective Observational Study

catalyst_summary: Shattuck Labs announced positive initial topline data from a Phase 1A/B dose expansion trial of SL-172154 in combination with azacitidine on December 13, 2023, showing a 79% objective response rate in frontline HR-MDS patients and 27% initial complete response in frontline TP53m AML patients.
ct_official_title: An Open-Label Phase 1a/1b Dose Escalation and Expansion Cohort Study of SL-172154 (SIRPα-Fc-CD40L) in Combination With Azacitidine or With Azacitidine and Venetoclax for the Treatment of Subjects With Higher-Risk Myelodysplastic Syndrome (MDS) or Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML)

catalyst_summary: PepGen announced clinical trial results from the 15 mg/kg cohort of the FREEDOM-DM1 Phase 1 trial, reporting a mean splicing correction of 53.7% in DM1 patients around late 2025, including November 13, 2025, but no specific announcement on or around September 25, 2025.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1 Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of Single-Ascending Doses of PGN-EDODM1 in Adult Participants With Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 (FREEDOM-DM1)

catalyst_summary: On February 27, 2024, Viking Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2 VENTURE trial results showing VK2735 achieved its primary endpoint with statistically significant body weight reductions up to 14.7% compared to placebo, with up to 88% of patients achieving at least 10% weight loss.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, 13-week Dose Finding Study of VK2735 Oral Formulation for Weight Management in Subjects Who Are Obese, or Overweight With at Least One Weight-related Comorbid Condition

catalyst_summary: ProMIS Neurosciences announced positive Phase 1a clinical trial results for PMN310 on July 17, 2023, showing the drug was well tolerated and demonstrated central nervous system exposure indicating target engagement in Alzheimer's disease.[3]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1b, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Multiple Ascending Dose Study of the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, and Preliminary Efficacy of PMN310 in Patients With Early Alzheimer's Disease

catalyst_summary: Invivyd announced interim exploratory clinical data from the ongoing Phase 3 CANOPY trial for VYD222 on or around December 2023, showing a potential early signal of clinical protection from symptomatic COVID-19 with 0% infection rate in the VYD222 arm versus 3% in placebo as of December 1, 2023 (median 67 days follow-up).[2]
ct_official_title: A Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of VYD222 for Prevention of COVID-19 (CANOPY)

catalyst_summary: On September 8, 2025, Rapport Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2a topline results for RAP-219 in drug-resistant focal onset seizures, showing 77.8% median seizure reduction and 24% seizure freedom.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of RAP-219 for the Acute Treatment of Manic Episodes, With or Without Mixed Features, Associated With Bipolar I Disorder

catalyst_summary: Cogent Biosciences (COGT) announced positive top-line Phase 3 PEAK trial results on November 10, 2025, showing bezuclastinib plus sunitinib reduced progression or death risk by 50% (HR 0.50) versus sunitinib alone in imatinib-resistant/intolerant GIST patients, with median PFS of 16.5 vs. 9.2 months and ORR of 46% vs. 26%.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3 Randomized, Open-Label, Multicenter Clinical Study of CGT9486+Sunitinib vs. Sunitinib in Subjects With Locally Advanced, Unresectable, or Metastatic Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors
ct_conditions: Advanced Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors; Metastatic Cancer

catalyst_summary: Phase III trial results for GMRx2, a novel triple combination pill (telmisartan, amlodipine, and indapamide) for hypertension, were published in The Lancet on October 18, 2024, showing superior blood pressure reduction and control rates compared to dual therapies with good tolerability.[1][4]
ct_official_title: Efficacy and Safety of GMRx2 (a Single Pill Combination Containing Telmisartan/Amlodipine/Indapamide) Compared to Placebo for the Treatment of Hypertension

catalyst_summary: Tectonic Therapeutic (TECX) announced positive interim data from the Phase 1b trial of TX45 in patients with Group 2 Pulmonary Hypertension in HFpEF on January 30, 2025.

Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) announced on March 31, 2025, that their Phase 3 ROSELLA trial of relacorilant plus nab-paclitaxel met its primary endpoint of improved progression-free survival (6.5 vs. 5.5 months, HR 0.70, p=0.008) in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer patients, with positive interim overall survival data (16.0 vs. 11.5 months, HR 0.69, p=0.012).[1][2]
ct_official_title: A Phase 3 Study of Relacorilant in Combination With Nab-Paclitaxel Versus Nab-Paclitaxel Monotherapy in Advanced, Platinum-Resistant, High-Grade Epithelial Ovarian, Primary Peritoneal, or Fallopian-Tube Cancer (ROSELLA)
ct_conditions: Ovarian Neoplasm; Fallopian Tube Neoplasms; Peritoneal Neoplasms

catalyst_summary: On August 7, 2023, Tango Therapeutics announced proof-of-mechanism data for TNG908 showing marked SDMA reduction in MTAP-deleted cancer cells, with 16 patients treated across dose escalation cohorts as of May 9, 2023.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2, Multi-Center, Open-Label Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Preliminary Anti-tumor Activity of TNG908 in Patients With MTAP-deleted Advanced or Metastatic Solid Tumors

catalyst_summary: Armata Pharmaceuticals presented late-breaking positive results from its Phase 2a diSArm study of AP-SA02, a Staphylococcus aureus bacteriophage cocktail, at IDWeek 2025 in October 2025, showing 88% clinical response rate at Day 12 versus 58% in placebo and zero non-response/relapse rates in the AP-SA02 group.
ct_official_title: Phase 1b/2a, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Multiple Ascending Dose Study of Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Intravenous AP-SA02 as an Adjunct to Best Available Antibiotic Therapy for the Treatment of Adults With Bacteremia Due to Staphylococcus Aureus
ct_conditions: Bacteremia; Staphylococcus Aureus; Staphylococcus Aureus Bacteremia; Bacteremia Staph; Bacteremia Due to Staphylococcus Aureus

catalyst_summary: Inhibrx announced positive topline results from the ChonDRAgon registrational trial on October 23, 2025, showing ozekibart achieved a 52% reduction in disease progression or death risk with median progression-free survival of 5.52 months versus 2.66 months for placebo in advanced chondrosarcoma.
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Blinded, Placebo-controlled, Phase 2 Study of INBRX-109 in Unresectable or Metastatic Conventional Chondrosarcoma

catalyst_summary: MBX Biosciences announced positive Phase 2 topline results for canvuparatide in hypoparathyroidism on September 22, 2025, showing a 63% responder rate at 12 weeks versus 31% placebo and 79% at six months in the open-label extension.

SAB Biotherapeutics announced positive Phase 3 trial results for SAB-185 on April 26, 2023, showing benefits in sustained symptom resolution for COVID-19 patients compared to REGEN-COV, though the primary endpoint was inconclusive due to low event rates.[1]
ct_official_title: Adaptive Platform Treatment Trial for Outpatients With COVID-19 (Adapt Out COVID)
ct_sponsor: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)

catalyst_summary: LP-10 phase 2a clinical trial top-line results were reported in January 2023.
ct_official_title: A Multicenter, Dose-Ranging Trial Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of LP-10 in Subjects With Symptomatic Oral Lichen Planus

catalyst_summary: Biomea Fusion announced initial positive topline data from the first two cohorts of patients with type 2 diabetes on March 28, 2023, showing that BMF-219, a menin inhibitor, demonstrated HbA1c reductions and improved beta cell function.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2 Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, PK, and PD of BMF-219, an Oral Covalent Menin Inhibitor, in Healthy Adults and Adults With T2D

catalyst_summary: Preliminary clinical trial results were published on Spruce Biosciences's website in August 2025 showing that TA-ERT reduced heparan sulfate to normal or near-normal levels over a five-year span and appeared to stop cognitive decline in patients with Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B (MPS IIIB).

C4 Therapeutics announced positive Phase 1 clinical trial data for CFT7455 in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma on December 12, 2023, showing it was well tolerated with evidence of anti-myeloma activity and immunomodulatory effects in heavily pretreated patients.[1][4]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2 Open-Label Multi-Center Study to Characterize the Safety and Tolerability of CFT7455 in Subjects With Relapsed/Refractory Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma or Multiple Myeloma

catalyst_summary: On September 26, 2023, Immunovant announced positive Phase 1 clinical trial results for IMVT-1402, showing that subcutaneous doses achieved IgG reductions similar to batoclimab with favorable safety profiles and no adverse effects on serum albumin or LDL cholesterol.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Multicenter, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind Study to Assess the Efficacy and Safety of IMVT-1402 in Patients With Mild to Severe Generalized Myasthenia Gravis

catalyst_summary: Innate Pharma announced updated Phase 1/2 clinical trial results for SAR443579/IPH6101 on or around December 13, 2024, showing clinical benefit with 5 complete remissions (4 CR/1 CRi) at 1 mg/kg and durable responses in R/R AML patients.[1]

Korro Bio announced disappointing Phase 1/2a REWRITE trial results for KRRO-110 in alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, showing the drug failed to reach projected protein levels in patients, prompting a strategic pivot to GalNAc delivery.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2a, Single- and Multiple-dose Escalation Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacodynamics, and Pharmacokinetics of KRRO 110 in Healthy Adult Volunteers and in Adult Participants With Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency (AATD) (REWRITE)

catalyst_summary: On or around August 7, 2023, Nektar announced corrected Phase 2b REZOLVE-AD clinical trial data for rezpegaldesleukin (rezpeg) in atopic dermatitis, showing improved efficacy metrics such as 36% placebo-adjusted EASI improvement for the high dose versus Lilly's prior 17% analysis.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2b, Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel-Group, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Rezpegaldesleukin in the Treatment of Adult Patients With Moderate-to-Severe Atopic Dermatitis (REZOLVE-AD)

catalyst_summary: Tectonic Therapeutic (TECX) announced favorable Phase 1a topline data for TX45 on or around June 21, 2024, demonstrating safety, tolerability, and favorable PK/PD profile in healthy volunteers, with no observed immunogenicity.[1]

No clinical trial data or results for ARTV (Artiva Biotherapeutics' AlloNK) were announced on or around December 15, 2025; the closest announcements were preliminary safety and translational data from 32 patients reported on November 12, 2025.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of AB-101, an Allogeneic Cord Blood- Derived NK-Cell Therapy in Combination With B-Cell Depleting mAb in Patients Who Failed Treatment for Class III or IV Lupus Nephritis or Other Forms of Refractory Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
ct_conditions: Lupus Nephritis - WHO Class III; Lupus Nephritis - WHO Class IV; Refractory Systemic Lupus Erythematosus; SLE

catalyst_summary: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) announced mixed Phase 3 VELA trial results for sonelokimab in hidradenitis suppurativa on September 28-29, 2025, where VELA-1 met its primary endpoint (HiSCR75 35% vs 18% placebo) but VELA-2 missed statistical significance (p=0.053) due to high placebo response, though significant under treatment policy strategy.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Parallel-group, Randomized, Double-blind, 3-arm, Placebo-controlled, Multicenter Study to Investigate the Efficacy and Safety of Subcutaneous Sonelokimab in Male and Female Participants Aged 18 Years and Over With Active Psoriatic Arthritis Who Are Naive to Biologic DMARDs

catalyst_summary: Avalo Therapeutics announced topline results from the Phase 2 PEAK trial of AVTX-002 (quisovalimab) in non-eosinophilic asthma on June 26, 2023, reporting that the trial failed to meet its primary endpoint of reducing asthma-related events, though the drug demonstrated a favorable safety profile and showed a trend toward fewer asthma events in patients with high baseline LIGHT levels.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Group Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of AVTX-002 for the Treatment of Poorly Controlled Non-Eosinophilic Asthma

catalyst_summary: Microbot Medical (MBOT) announced on January 3, 2025, the successful completion of its ACCESS-PVI pivotal human clinical trial for the LIBERTY Endovascular Robotic System, with full results detailed later on April 9, 2025, showing 100% robotic navigation success and 92% radiation reduction.[4][1]

Mereo BioPharma (MREO) announced on December 29, 2025, that the Phase 3 ORBIT and COSMIC studies for setrusumab in Osteogenesis Imperfecta showed significant BMD improvements but failed to meet primary endpoints on fracture rates.[1][2][5]
ct_official_title: An Operationally Seamless, Randomized Phase 2/3 Study Consisting of a Phase 2 Single-Blind, Dose-Evaluation Phase and a Phase 3 Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase to Assess the Efficacy and Safety of Setrusumab in Subjects With Osteogenesis Imperfecta

catalyst_summary: Aclaris Therapeutics (ACRS) announced top-line results on November 13, 2023, for its Phase 2b clinical trial of zunsemetinib in rheumatoid arthritis, which failed to meet the primary endpoint of ACR20 response or any secondary endpoints at both 20mg and 50mg doses.
ct_official_title: Phase 2b, Randomized, Multicenter, Double-blind, Parallel Group, Placebo Controlled, Dose Ranging Study to Investigate the Efficacy, Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of Multiple Doses of ATI-450 Plus Methotrexate (MTX) Versus Placebo Plus MTX in Patients With Moderate to Severe Active Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA) Who Have Had an Inadequate Response to MTX Alone

catalyst_summary: Kazia Therapeutics announced Phase II/III trial results for paxalisib in glioblastoma on July 10, 2024, showing a 3.8-month improvement in overall survival for newly diagnosed unmethylated patients.
ct_official_title: GBM AGILE: Global Adaptive Trial Master Protocol: An International, Seamless Phase II/III Response Adaptive Randomization Platform Trial Designed To Evaluate Multiple Regimens In Newly Diagnosed and Recurrent GBM

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for PALI-2108 were announced on or around January 29, 2024; available announcements report positive preliminary Phase 1a/b data later in 2024 and 2025, including safety, tolerability, and efficacy in ulcerative colitis patients.[1][2][3]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics Study of PALI-2108 in Healthy Volunteers, With an Open-Label Study of a Patient Cohort With Ulcerative Colitis and a Phase 1b, Open-Label, Cohort in Patients With Fibrostenosing Crohn's Disease

catalyst_summary: Spruce Biosciences (SPRB) announced integrated long-term clinical data from studies 201, 202, 401, and 902 for tralesinidase alfa (TA-ERT) in Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B (MPS IIIB), showing rapid reductions in disease biomarker heparan sulfate, stabilization of cognition, communication, motor skills, and cortical grey matter volume compared to declines in untreated patients.[1][2]

Cytokinetics announced positive topline results from the SEQUOIA-HCM Phase 3 clinical trial of aficamten in patients with obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy on December 27, 2023, showing statistically significant improvements in exercise capacity and all secondary endpoints.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Multi-Center, Randomized, Double-Blind Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Aficamten Compared to Placebo in Adults With Symptomatic Non-Obstructive Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy

catalyst_summary: Relmada Therapeutics presented positive Phase 2 clinical trial data for NDV-01 on April 28, 2025, showing an 85% overall response rate at 3 months and 90% of patients achieving high-grade disease-free status in non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of NDV-01 Versus Observation in Participants With Intermediate-risk Non-muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer (BOOST)
ct_conditions: Bladder (Urothelial, Transitional Cell) Cancer Superficial (Non-Invasive); Bladder (Urothelial, Transitional Cell) Cancer; Urothelial Carcinoma Bladder; Urologic Cancer

catalyst_summary: On March 8, 2024, Amylyx Pharmaceuticals announced topline results from the Phase 3 PHOENIX trial showing that AMX0035 failed to meet its primary and secondary endpoints in treating ALS, with no statistically significant difference compared to placebo.
ct_official_title: A Phase IIIb, Open Label Extension Study Evaluating The Safety And Tolerability of AMX0035 Up To 108 Weeks In Adult Participants With Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) Previously Enrolled In Study A35-004 (PHOENIX)

catalyst_summary: Biosyngen presented next-generation TIL technology data at the ESMO 2024 Annual Meeting in September 2024, with BRG01 in Phase II trials for solid tumors and BST02 and BRL03 in Phase I trials.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1 Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of BRG01 in Subjects With Relapsed/Metastatic EBV-positive Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma

catalyst_summary: Elicio Therapeutics (ELTX) announced positive interim Phase 1 data for ELI-002 2P on June 2-6, 2023, showing it was well-tolerated with no dose-limiting toxicities, 77% tumor biomarker reduction, 32% clearance, and robust mKRAS-specific T cell responses in 87% of patients with high-relapse-risk pancreatic and colorectal cancers.[1]
ct_official_title: First in Human Phase 1/2 Trial of ELI-002 7P Immunotherapy as Treatment for Subjects With Kirsten Rat Sarcoma (KRAS)/Neuroblastoma RAS Viral Oncogene Homolog (NRAS) Mutated Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma (PDAC) and Other Solid Tumors
ct_conditions: Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma; Colorectal Cancer; KRAS G12D; KRAS G12R; KRAS G12V; KRAS G12A; KRAS G12C; KRAS G12S; KRAS G13D; NRAS G12D; NRAS G12R; NRAS G12V; NRAS G12C; NRAS G12S

catalyst_summary: Neumora Therapeutics (NMRA) announced topline results from the Phase 3 KOASTAL-1 study of navacaprant in major depressive disorder on January 2, 2025, which failed to meet the primary endpoint of MADRS total score reduction and key secondary endpoint compared to placebo, though showing an efficacy signal in female participants.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Effects of Oral NMRA-335140 Versus Placebo in Participants With Major Depressive Disorder

catalyst_summary: AVTX-002 (quisovalimab) Phase 2 PEAK trial in non-eosinophilic asthma did not meet its primary endpoint for reduction in asthma-related events, though positive trends were observed in a patient subpopulation with elevated baseline LIGHT levels.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel Group Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of AVTX-002 for the Treatment of Poorly Controlled Non-Eosinophilic Asthma

catalyst_summary: Ventyx Biosciences announced Phase 2 SERENITY trial results for VTX958 in moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis on November 6-7, 2023, meeting primary and key secondary endpoints but with efficacy below internal targets, leading to termination of development in psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.[1][2][3][5]
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Group Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of VTX958 in Participants With Moderate to Severe Psoriasis

catalyst_summary: On October 31, 2023, TuHURA Biosciences announced preliminary topline results for VAL-083 from the GBM AGILE study, which did not perform better than the standard of care.[8]
ct_official_title: GBM AGILE: Global Adaptive Trial Master Protocol: An International, Seamless Phase II/III Response Adaptive Randomization Platform Trial Designed To Evaluate Multiple Regimens In Newly Diagnosed and Recurrent GBM

catalyst_summary: Korro Bio announced clinical trial results for KRRO-110 in AATD patients on November 12, 2025, showing functional M-AAT protein production but below projected levels (peak ~2-3 µM increase vs. 11 µM threshold) as of a November 6 data cutoff, leading to stock drop and strategic pivot.[1][2][5]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2a, Single- and Multiple-dose Escalation Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacodynamics, and Pharmacokinetics of KRRO 110 in Healthy Adult Volunteers and in Adult Participants With Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency (AATD) (REWRITE)

catalyst_summary: Clinical trial results for ADI-PEG 20 (pegargiminase, also known as ATOMIC-Meso) in mesothelioma were published in 2024, showing improved median overall survival of 9.3 months versus 7.7 months with chemotherapy alone, though no specific announcement on or around March 26, 2024, is detailed in available sources.
ct_official_title: Genotype Selected, Randomized, Open Label, Phase 2 Study of ADI-PEG 20 Plus Lenvatinib Treatment in Subjects With Unresectable Hepatocellular Carcinoma

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for LONA were announced on or around 2024-09-04; the closest match is Longeveron's Phase 2b trial results for laromestrocel (Lomecel-B) in age-related frailty, published October 29, 2024, showing a 63.4-meter improvement in 6-minute walk test at month 9 (p=0.0077).

No clinical trial data or results for SPRY (Spry Belt device for osteopenia) were announced on or around 2023-05-12; the referenced UNMC article from January 2023 discusses an ongoing study without specific results on that date.[4][6]

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) announced positive topline 12-week results from the Phase 2 MIRA trial of sonelokimab in hidradenitis suppurativa on June 25, 2023, meeting the primary HiSCR75 endpoint with 43.3% and 34.8% response rates for 120mg and 240mg doses versus 14.7% placebo.[1][4]

catalyst_summary: On December 4, 2024, Relmada Therapeutics (RLMD) announced that the Data Monitoring Committee determined the Phase 3 Reliance II trial of REL-1017 was futile and unlikely to meet its primary efficacy endpoint, with no new safety concerns identified.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Multicenter, Open-Label Study to Assess the Long-Term Safety of REL-1017 as a Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder

catalyst_summary: BioAge Labs announced on December 6, 2024, the discontinuation of its STRIDES Phase 2 clinical trial for azelaprag in combination with tirzepatide due to unexpected liver transaminitis observed in some patients, with no full efficacy results released.

catalyst_summary: Cumberland Pharmaceuticals (CPIX) announced positive top-line results from the Phase 2 FIGHT DMD trial of ifetroban on February 4, 2025, showing significant improvements in left ventricular ejection fraction and reductions in cardiac damage markers in DMD patients.

catalyst_summary: Annovis Bio (ANVS) announced positive Phase III Parkinson's disease study results for buntanetap on July 2, 2024, showing statistically significant improvements in MDS-UPDRS scores, motor/non-motor functions, and cognition, particularly in patients with longer disease duration and PIGD.

catalyst_summary: Q32 Bio (QTTB) announced Phase 2a clinical trial results for bempikibart in alopecia areata (SIGNAL-AA) and atopic dermatitis (SIGNAL-AD) on or around December 11, 2024, showing mixed outcomes with failure to meet primary endpoints, high placebo responses, and protocol issues leading to sharp stock declines.

catalyst_summary: Armata Pharmaceuticals (ARMP) announced positive topline results from the Phase 1b/2a diSArm trial of AP-SA02 on May 19, 2025, showing 88% clinical response rate at day 12 versus 58% for placebo, with 100% response at later points and no serious adverse events.[2][3][4][7]

catalyst_summary: Wave Life Sciences announced positive proof-of-mechanism clinical trial data from the RestorAATion-2 study of WVE-006 on October 16, 2024, demonstrating the first-ever therapeutic RNA editing in humans with wild-type M-AAT protein reaching over 60% of total AAT in the first two patients after a single dose.

catalyst_summary: Unicycive announced the successful completion of the UNI-494 Phase 1 clinical trial in healthy volunteers in their third quarter 2024 financial results, stating it was well-tolerated up to 160 mg single dose and 40 mg twice daily multiple doses, with fast absorption and metabolism to nicorandil.[1]

Tonix Pharmaceuticals (TNXP) announced topline results from the Phase 2 PREVAIL proof-of-concept study of TNX-102 SL for fibromyalgia-type Long COVID on September 5, 2023, showing a robust effect size of 0.5 in improving fatigue and positive trends in secondary endpoints despite missing the primary pain reduction endpoint.
